export default abstract class Server<
  ServerOptions extends Options = Options,
  ServerRequest extends BaseNextRequest = BaseNextRequest,
  ServerResponse extends BaseNextResponse = BaseNextResponse,
> {
  public readonly hostname?: string
  public readonly fetchHostname?: string
  public readonly port?: number
  protected readonly dir: string
  protected readonly quiet: boolean
  protected readonly nextConfig: NextConfigRuntime
  protected readonly distDir: string
  protected readonly publicDir: string
  protected readonly hasStaticDir: boolean
  protected readonly pagesManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly appPathsManifest?: PagesManifest
  protected readonly buildId: string
  protected readonly deploymentId: string
  protected readonly dev: boolean
  protected readonly minimalMode: boolean
  protected readonly renderOpts: BaseRenderOpts
  protected readonly serverOptions: Readonly<ServerOptions>
  protected readonly appPathRoutes?: Record<string, string[]>
  protected readonly clientReferenceManifest?: DeepReadonly<ClientReferenceManifest>
  protected interceptionRoutePatterns: RegExp[]
  protected nextFontManifest?: DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
  protected instrumentation: InstrumentationModule | undefined

  protected abstract getPublicDir(): string
  protected abstract getHasStaticDir(): boolean
  protected abstract getPagesManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getAppPathsManifest(): PagesManifest | undefined
  protected abstract getBuildId(): string
  protected abstract getinterceptionRoutePatterns(): RegExp[]

  protected readonly enabledDirectories: NextEnabledDirectories
  protected abstract getEnabledDirectories(dev: boolean): NextEnabledDirectories

  protected readonly experimentalTestProxy?: boolean

  protected abstract findPageComponents(params: {
    locale: string | undefined
    page: string
    query: NextParsedUrlQuery
    params: Params
    isAppPath: boolean
    // The following parameters are used in the development server's
    // implementation.
    sriEnabled?: boolean
    appPaths?: ReadonlyArray<string> | null
    shouldEnsure?: boolean
    url?: string
  }): Promise<FindComponentsResult | null>
  protected abstract getPrerenderManifest(): DeepReadonly<PrerenderManifest>
  protected abstract getNextFontManifest():
    | DeepReadonly<NextFontManifest>
    | undefined
  protected abstract attachRequestMeta(
    req: ServerRequest,
    parsedUrl: NextUrlWithParsedQuery
  ): void
  protected abstract hasPage(pathname: string): Promise<boolean>

  protected abstract sendRenderResult(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    options: {
      result: RenderResult
      generateEtags: boolean
      poweredByHeader: boolean
      cacheControl: CacheControl | undefined
    }
  ): Promise<void>

  protected abstract runApi(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    query: ParsedUrlQuery,
    match: PagesAPIRouteMatch
  ): Promise<boolean>

  protected abstract renderHTML(
    req: ServerRequest,
    res: ServerResponse,
    pathname: string,
    query: NextParsedUrlQuery,
    renderOpts: LoadedRenderOpts
  ): Promise<RenderResult>

  protected abstract getIncrementalCache(options: {
    requestHeaders: Record<string, undefined | string | string[]>
  }): Promise<import('./lib/incremental-cache').IncrementalCache>

  protected getServerComponentsHmrCache():
    | ServerComponentsHmrCache
    | undefined {
    return this.nextConfig.experimental.serverComponentsHmrCache
      ? (globalThis as any).__serverComponentsHmrCache
      : undefined
  }

  protected abstract loadEnvConfig(params: {
    dev: boolean
    forceReload: boolean
  }): void

  // TODO-APP: (wyattjoh): Make protected again. Used for turbopack in route-resolver.ts right now.
  public readonly matchers: RouteMatcherManager
  protected readonly i18nProvider?: I18NProvider
  protected readonly localeNormalizer?: LocaleRouteNormalizer

  protected readonly normalizers: {
    readonly rsc: RSCPathnameNormalizer | undefined
    readonly segmentPrefetchRSC: SegmentPrefixRSCPathnameNormalizer | undefined
    readonly data: NextDataPathnameNormalizer | undefined
  }

  private readonly isAppPPREnabled: boolean

  /**

872	  public logError(err: unknown): void {
873	    if (this.quiet) return
874	    Log.error(err)
875	  }
876	
877	  public async handleRequest(
878	    req: ServerRequest,
879	    res: ServerResponse,
880	    parsedUrl?: NextUrlWithParsedQuery
881	  ): Promise<void> {
882	    await this.prepare()
883	    const method = req.method.toUpperCase()
884	    const tracer = getTracer()
885	
886	    return tracer.withPropagatedContext(req.headers, () => {
887	      // Capture the parent span before creating the handleRequest span.
888	      // When deployed with an adapter, the platform's runtime may create its
889	      // own OTEL HTTP server span before Next.js runs. We propagate http.route
890	      // to this parent span so APM tools (e.g. Datadog) can derive the
891	      // resource name correctly.
892	      const parentSpan = tracer.getActiveScopeSpan()
893	
894	      return tracer.trace(
895	        BaseServerSpan.handleRequest,
896	        {
897	          spanName: `${method}`,
898	          kind: SpanKind.SERVER,
899	          attributes: {
900	            'http.method': method,
901	            'http.target': req.url,
902	          },
903	        },
904	        async (span) =>
905	          this.handleRequestImpl(req, res, parsedUrl).finally(() => {
906	            if (!span) return
907	
908	            const isRSCRequest = getRequestMeta(req, 'isRSCRequest') ?? false
909	            span.setAttributes({
910	              'http.status_code': res.statusCode,
911	              'next.rsc': isRSCRequest,
912	            })
913	
914	            if (res.statusCode && res.statusCode >= 500) {
915	              // For 5xx status codes: SHOULD be set to 'Error' span status.
916	              // x-ref: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/http/http-spans/#status
917	              span.setStatus({
918	                code: SpanStatusCode.ERROR,
919	              })
920	              // For span status 'Error', SHOULD set 'error.type' attribute.
921	              span.setAttribute('error.type', res.statusCode.toString())
922	            }
923	
924	            const rootSpanAttributes = tracer.getRootSpanAttributes()
925	            // We were unable to get attributes, probably OTEL is not enabled
926	            if (!rootSpanAttributes) return
927	
928	            if (
929	              rootSpanAttributes.get('next.span_type') !==
930	              BaseServerSpan.handleRequest
931	            ) {
932	              console.warn(
933	                `Unexpected root span type '${rootSpanAttributes.get(
934	                  'next.span_type'
935	                )}'. Please report this Next.js issue https://github.com/vercel/next.js`
936	              )
937	              return
938	            }
939	
940	            const route = rootSpanAttributes.get('next.route')
941	            if (route) {
942	              const name = isRSCRequest
943	                ? `RSC ${method} ${route}`
944	                : `${method} ${route}`
945	
946	              span.setAttributes({
947	                'next.route': route,
948	                'http.route': route,
949	                'next.span_name': name,
950	              })
951	              span.updateName(name)
952	
953	              // Propagate http.route to the parent span if one exists and
954	              // is different from the handleRequest span. This ensures APM
955	              // tools that read attributes from the outermost span (e.g.
956	              // a platform-created HTTP span) can derive the resource name.
957	              if (parentSpan && parentSpan !== span) {
958	                parentSpan.setAttribute('http.route', route)
959	              }
960	            } else {
961	              span.updateName(isRSCRequest ? `RSC ${method}` : `${method}`)
